_doc: Real Etsy winners with verified monthly revenue. Used to populate strategy.realWorldExample with confidence (no AI hallucination) and as inspiration for what's working in each category. is_recommended=false entries are kept as cautionary examples — they earn well but face IP enforcement risk.

winners.name: Amber Table Lamp
winners.why_it_sells: Premium aesthetic lamp; warm amber glow is on-trend in home decor; very high perceived value.
winners.differentiator: Highest sustained monthly revenue in this list. Complex print commands premium price; photography is make-or-break.

winners.name: Crossbow Dice Roller and Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Mechanical design with high perceived value; DnD community is passionate and high-spending.
winners.differentiator: Original mechanical design — no IP risk. Excellent bundle potential with dice sets.

winners.name: Mystic Conch Shell Dice Storage
winners.why_it_sells: Unique decorative gaming accessory; high perceived value; dice storage inside adds function.
winners.differentiator: Original design with no IP risk. Strong photography potential. Excellent bundle piece with a dice set.

winners.name: Personalized Headphone Stand
winners.why_it_sells: Premium desk accessory + personalisation = strong gift; popular with gamers and WFH buyers.
winners.differentiator: Custom name engraving justifies premium price; Q4 gift spike. Same product from another seller earned 5–10× less, proving photography and SEO determine outcome.

winners.name: The Glitch Fidget Slider
winners.why_it_sells: Mechanical fidget satisfies tactile need; professional aesthetic appeals to adults not just kids.
winners.differentiator: Original multi-layer mechanism — hard to replicate without engineering skill. Two sellers with the same design had 5× revenue gap based on listing quality alone.

winners.name: Water Dispenser Lever (Replacement)
winners.why_it_sells: Solves a frustrating real problem; exact-fit replacement; near-zero competition.
winners.differentiator: Possibly the highest return per gram of filament in this list — simple print with massive demand. Strongest argument for the replacement parts niche.

winners.name: Break Glass in Case of Emergency
winners.why_it_sells: Humorous novelty; perfect low-cost gift; huge impulse-buy appeal.
winners.differentiator: Low filament cost; extremely fast print; viral social media potential.

winners.name: Mini TV Apple Watch Charging Station
winners.why_it_sells: Solves a real problem elegantly; nostalgic mini-TV form factor makes it highly giftable.
winners.differentiator: Unique form factor with strong gifting angle; evergreen as long as Apple Watch sells.

winners.name: HomePod Mini Outlet Stand
winners.why_it_sells: Saves desk space; outlet-mount is a clever functional solve; Apple ecosystem buyers are loyal.
winners.differentiator: Niche but loyal buyer base; low print time; functional need with no Amazon alternative. v2 listing earned 3× more than v1 — better photography/SEO is the difference.
winners.notes: Avoid Apple logo or 'Designed for Apple' phrasing.

winners.name: Flipper Zero WiFi Dev Board Case
winners.why_it_sells: Exact-fit case for a cult hacker/maker device; passionate technical community with disposable income.
winners.differentiator: No IP risk; niche maker audience is loyal and active; low Etsy competition; evergreen as long as Flipper Zero sells.

winners.name: Designer Toilet Paper Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Functional replacement product with design angle; every bathroom needs one.
winners.differentiator: Low competition for unique shapes; replacement market = high buyer urgency.

winners.name: Capybara Dice Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Trending animal meets DnD accessory; double-niche drives strong conversion from two audiences.
winners.differentiator: Trend-aware design in an evergreen niche; pairs well with a matching dice tray for AOV uplift.

winners.name: Gas Can Christmas Ornament
winners.why_it_sells: Petrolhead gifting; strong humour and novelty angle; Christmas ornament seasonal spike.
winners.differentiator: Q4 spike (Oct–Dec); low filament cost; obvious gift for car/truck enthusiasts with near-zero competition.

winners.name: iPhone MagSafe Weighted Ball Charger Stand
winners.why_it_sells: Premium feel; weighted base prevents tipping; clean minimal aesthetic.
winners.differentiator: Material weight = perceived premium. MagSafe market is evergreen with each new iPhone generation. Older listing still converting strongly proves sustained demand.
winners.notes: Avoid Apple/MagSafe logos in product photos.

winners.name: KitchenAid Tool Holder Set of 3
winners.why_it_sells: Exact fit for a beloved premium appliance; KitchenAid owners are loyal and high-income.
winners.differentiator: Buyers of a €300+ appliance pay for quality. Set of 3 boosts AOV significantly. Low IP risk: avoid the KitchenAid wordmark and rely on 'fits stand mixers' phrasing.
winners.notes: Use generic phrasing like 'fits stand mixer'; do not imply official affiliation.

winners.name: Ford F-150 MagSafe Phone Mount
winners.why_it_sells: Exact-fit vehicle mount; F-150 is the best-selling vehicle in the USA — huge addressable market.
winners.differentiator: Hyper-niche exact fit = near-zero competition. Strong repeat angle for truck owners upgrading phones.
winners.notes: Avoid Blue Oval or F-150 logo in photos. Use 'fits 2015–2020 F-150' phrasing.

winners.name: Dyson Accessory Holders (set of 6)
winners.why_it_sells: Exact-fit for a premium Dyson vacuum; organises expensive accessories buyers already own.
winners.differentiator: Set of 6 = high AOV; Dyson owners are high-income and loyal; evergreen as long as Dyson sells.
winners.notes: Dyson does not actively pursue third-party accessory makers. Avoid 'Dyson' in title; use 'fits V8/V10/V11'.

winners.name: Hello Neighbour Lock and Key Puzzle
winners.why_it_sells: Playful puzzle mechanic; satisfying unboxing; strong gift appeal.
winners.differentiator: Mechanical design moat — hard to copy without understanding the mechanism.

winners.name: Baby First Mjolnir (Thor Hammer Rattle)
winners.why_it_sells: Clever mashup: baby rattle + Marvel reference = perfect geek parent gift; highly shareable.
winners.differentiator: Geeky parent niche is underserved and willing to pay. Mjolnir is a grey-area IP because of Norse mythology origin.
winners.notes: Generic rounded-hammer design with no Marvel logo is lower risk than MCU-style replicas.

winners.name: Apple HomePod Mini + Echo Dot Combo Stand
winners.why_it_sells: Solves multi-device desk clutter; appeals to smart home buyers who own both devices.
winners.differentiator: Combo stand = premium price justification. Smart home market growing; differentiated from single-device stands.

winners.name: Mini Barbell Squat Rack Pen Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Gym culture novelty gift; instantly shareable; obvious gifting angle for fitness lovers.
winners.differentiator: Very fast print; low filament cost; strong social media shareability; evergreen gym community.

winners.name: Dyson Vacuum Adapter for Ruggable Rug
winners.why_it_sells: Solves a specific Dyson + Ruggable incompatibility; very targeted buyer with no other option.
winners.differentiator: Near-zero competition; buyer is motivated and price-tolerant; exact-problem solve.

winners.name: MagSafe Desk Mount
winners.why_it_sells: Clean minimal desk organisation; MagSafe is Apple ecosystem standard used by millions.
winners.differentiator: Functional evergreen product; low filament cost; easy to photograph cleanly on white desk.

winners.name: Raised Slow-Feeder Pet Bowl
winners.why_it_sells: Functional pet health accessory; vet-recommended slow feeding is a growing buyer-driven trend.
winners.differentiator: Underserved sub-niche. Use food-safe PETG or resin and add a clear material disclaimer to overcome the FDM-PLA-not-food-safe objection.
winners.notes: Food contact concern — must be PETG/resin not standard PLA, with disclaimer on listing.

winners.name: Treasure Chest Nintendo Switch Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Clever treasure chest form for Switch storage; gaming nostalgia drives gifting.
winners.differentiator: Chest form factor without any Nintendo logo is the safer way to capture this audience.
winners.notes: Avoid Nintendo or Switch branding in title; use 'console storage' phrasing.

winners.name: Personalized Cat Food Bowl (Small)
winners.why_it_sells: Custom name adds emotional value; pet owners are highly motivated buyers.
winners.differentiator: Personalisation premium; repeat buyer potential for multi-pet households.
winners.notes: Food contact concern — PLA is not food-safe. Use PETG or add a clear product disclaimer.

winners.name: Little Tikes Rear Numberplate (Replacement)
winners.why_it_sells: Exact replacement for a discontinued toy part; parents desperate to fix a beloved toy.
winners.differentiator: Near-zero competition; buyer urgency is maximum. Strongest argument for the replacement parts niche.
winners.notes: Functional replacement parts are typically tolerated. Avoid Little Tikes wordmark in title.

winners.name: Personalized Charging Cord Organiser
winners.why_it_sells: Personalisation adds gifting value to a mundane object; desk organisation trend ongoing.
winners.differentiator: Low filament cost; easy to customise per order; stocking-filler price point boosts Q4 volume.

winners.name: Tesla Model 3/Y Sunglasses Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Exact-fit interior accessory; Tesla owners are passionate and high-income.
winners.differentiator: Loyal niche community; works in both Model 3 and Y — doubles addressable market; low competition.
winners.notes: Avoid Tesla wordmark or logo in listing title or photos.

winners.name: Jack Stand Phone Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Novelty form factor (car jack) appeals to automotive hobbyists; low price = impulse buy.
winners.differentiator: Concept has more potential with better photography and SEO — still an early-stage listing.

winners.name: Tesla Supercharger Phone Charger Stand
winners.why_it_sells: Nostalgic Tesla Supercharger form factor on a functional object; strong Tesla community gifting.
winners.differentiator: Tesla fandom is active and high-income. Strong gifting angle.
winners.notes: Don't use the Supercharger or Tesla logo. Sell as 'EV charger inspired phone stand'.

winners.name: Snorlax Apple Watch Charger Cover
winners.why_it_sells: Character shape matches Apple Watch puck perfectly; Pokémon fandom is massive and buying.
winners.differentiator: High earner but built on Nintendo IP — listing can be removed at any time. Use as evidence of demand for the format, not as a model to copy.
winners.notes: Cautionary example — Nintendo enforcement is aggressive. Sell an original character instead of copying this.

winners.name: Simpsons Sponge Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Homer's mouth is the perfect sponge slot; viral social share potential; highly recognisable.
winners.differentiator: Strong revenue but one DMCA = shop gone. Use as proof that the 'character mouth holds object' format works — design an original character around the same concept.
winners.notes: Cautionary example. Original character with a similar form factor is the safer play.

winners.name: Super Mario Thwomp Tissue Box Cover
winners.why_it_sells: Viral gaming decor; instantly recognisable character; functional novelty for gamer homes.
winners.differentiator: Nintendo is the most aggressive IP enforcer on Etsy — high suspension risk. Demonstrates demand for 'gaming character as functional household object', not a template to copy.
winners.notes: Cautionary example. Build an original character/creature around the same concept.

winners.name: Avengers Tower Amazon Echo Dot Holder
winners.why_it_sells: Iconic architecture fits Echo Dot perfectly; Marvel fans are passionate and high-spending.
winners.differentiator: Very high revenue potential but suspension risk is real. Format works for any tall iconic landmark.
winners.notes: Cautionary example. Try a generic skyscraper or original architecture instead.

winners.name: Amazon Echo Dot R2-D2 Accessory
winners.why_it_sells: Star Wars fandom; R2-D2 form factor matches Echo Dot shape perfectly.
winners.differentiator: High IP risk. Disney actively enforces Star Wars on Etsy. Use as proof that 'cylindrical character on Echo Dot' works — design an original robot.

winners.name: Mickey Apple Watch Charger Cover
winners.why_it_sells: Character-branded tech accessory; strong gifting angle for Disney fans.
winners.differentiator: High IP risk (Disney). Etsy ban likely if flagged. Demand exists — capture it with an original character.

winners.name: Lightsaber Wall Mount
winners.why_it_sells: Display mount for prop collectors; collectors pay well for quality display solutions.
winners.differentiator: Etsy actively removes Star Wars listings. Generic 'sword display mount' captures most of the same buyers.

winners.name: Baby Rattle Lightsaber
winners.why_it_sells: Geek parent gifting angle; Star Wars fandom is huge.
winners.differentiator: High IP risk (Disney/Lucasfilm). Lower revenue than the equivalent Mjolnir rattle confirms Star Wars enforcement is stricter.

winners.name: Captain Underpants Hypno Ring
winners.why_it_sells: IP-adjacent novelty; low price = impulse buy.
winners.differentiator: Low margin combined with IP risk makes this a poor recommendation. Original-character novelty rings serve the same impulse-buy buyer.
